title: parasenecio × shirouma-montanus
intro: Parasenecio × shirouma-montanus is a sterile hybrid species of flowering plant, resulting from the cross between Senecio shiroumensis and Senecio montanus. It is native to Japan and is known for its unique properties and medicinal uses. The plant has been found to contain compounds with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, often dried and processed into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity associated with certain species within the Senecio genus.
